meta.description: The universal root node's type. The single root item (all-zeros UUID) is a well-known anchor whose payload is the datastore's authoritative configuration record (owner, spec version, history/activity modes, entry point) — there is no separate config file or table. root is both a well-known lifecycle anchor and a projected structured type: its payload projects to obj_<root-type> like any other type.

owner.description: Human-readable identifier for who owns or manages this datastore. Free string. Used in Studio headers and sync conflict resolution.

specVersion.description: Kanecta spec version this datastore was last written by (e.g. "1.4.0"). Read at open time; migration tooling uses it to determine needed migrations.

itemHistory.description: Whether item_history items are written on create/update. NONE = off; ITEM = stored inside items/; EXTERNAL = routed to a sibling item-history store. Default EXTERNAL.

activity.description: Whether activity items are written for workspace events. NONE = off; ITEM = stored inside items/; EXTERNAL = routed to a sibling activity store. Default EXTERNAL.

entryPoint.description: UUID of the view item that bootstraps this deployment in Studio. Null = Studio falls back to rendering the root node directly.

sqlSchema: -- Built-in type: adapter handles storage natively.
